description_long: This object is a classic cast iron radiator, featuring a series of vertically aligned, rounded columns connected side by side. Each column is hollow, allowing for the circulation of hot water or steam, and the overall structure is robust and solid, typical of traditional radiators found in older urban buildings. The surface appears to be coated in a dark, matte finish, likely to resist corrosion and blend with industrial or utilitarian interiors. The radiator has a symmetrical, modular design, with each fin or column repeating uniformly across its length. There are no visible decorative elements, focusing on functionality and heat dissipation. Pipe connection points are implied at the ends, but no valves or controls are distinctly visible in the images.
description: A dark, matte-finished cast iron radiator with a series of vertical, rounded columns and a robust, utilitarian design. The structure is modular and symmetrical, emphasizing function over decoration.
description_view_0: The radiator is seen from the end, showing the oval opening of a single column and the hollow interior.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full length of the radiator, displaying all vertical columns in a row and the uniform, repeating pattern.
description_view_2: The opposite end view mirrors view 0, again showing the oval opening and hollow structure of a single column.
description_view_3: Another side view, identical to view 1, showing the full array of vertical columns and the radiator's length.
attribute_pipe_connections: dual entry, one at each end, horizontally oriented
